citations.title: Alcoholic fermentation: the regulation of Saccharomyces cerevisiae glycolytic genes
The Crabtree effect: a regulatory system in yeast

citations.authors: Abbott DA, Zelle RM, Pronk JT, van Maris AJ
citations.title: Metabolic engineering of Saccharomyces cerevisiae for production of carboxylic acids

amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Alcoholic fermentation in Saccharomyces cerevisiae is the metabolic pathway that converts glucose to ethanol and CO2 under anaerobic conditions. This process involves glycolysis followed by ethanolic fermentation, and is regulated by oxygen availability (Crabtree effect), glucose concentration, and energy status. Yeast preferentially ferments even in the presence of oxygen when glucose is abundant, making this pathway central to brewing, baking, and biofuel production.

Trigger?} D --> G F --> G G -->|Yes| H[Activate Fermentation Pathway] G -->|No| I[Favor Respiration] %% Glucose Transport H --> J[Glucose Transporters HXT1-7] J --> K[Glucose Entry into Cytoplasm] K --> L[Intracellular Glucose] %% Glycolysis Phase 1: Energy Investment L --> M[Hexokinase HXK1/HXK2] M --> N[Glucose-6-Phosphate] N --> O[Phosphoglucose Isomerase PGI1] O --> P[Fructose-6-Phosphate] P --> Q[Phosphofructokinase PFK1/PFK2] Q --> R[Fructose-1,6-Bisphosphate] %% Glycolysis Phase 2: Cleavage R --> S[Aldolase FBA1] S --> T[Dihydroxyacetone Phosphate DHAP] S --> U[Glyceraldehyde-3-Phosphate G3P] T --> V[Triose Phosphate Isomerase TPI1] V --> U %% Glycolysis Phase 3: Energy Payoff U --> W[Glyceraldehyde-3-P Dehydrogenase TDH1-3] W --> X[1,3-Bisphosphoglycerate] X --> Y[Phosphoglycerate Kinase PGK1] Y --> Z[3-Phosphoglycerate + ATP] Z --> AA[Phosphoglycerate Mutase GPM1-3] AA --> AB[2-Phosphoglycerate] AB --> AC[Enolase ENO1/ENO2] AC --> AD[Phosphoenolpyruvate PEP] AD --> AE[Pyruvate Kinase CDC19/PYK2] AE --> AF[Pyruvate + ATP] %% Regulatory Decision Point AF --> AG{OR: Oxygen Available
for Respiration?} D --> AG AG -->|No O2| AH[Commit to Fermentation] AG -->|O2 Present but
High Glucose| AH AG -->|O2 + Low Glucose| AI[Pyruvate to Mitochondria] %% Fermentation Branch AH --> AJ[Pyruvate Decarboxylase PDC1/PDC5/PDC6] AJ --> AK[Acetaldehyde + CO2] %% NAD+ Regeneration Critical Step AK --> AL{AND: Acetaldehyde +
NADH Available?} W --> AM[NADH Production from Glycolysis] AM --> AL AL -->|Yes| AN[Alcohol Dehydrogenase ADH1/ADH2] AN --> AO(\1) AN --> AP[NAD+ Regenerated] %% NAD+ Recycling Loop AP --> AQ[NAD+ Returns to Glycolysis] AQ --> W %% ATP Balance and Energy Z --> AR[Net ATP Production] AE --> AR M --> AS[ATP Consumed in Early Glycolysis] Q --> AS AR --> AT{Is ATP Sufficient
for Growth?} AT -->|Yes| AU[Maintain Fermentation Rate] AT -->|No| AV[Increase Glucose Uptake] AV --> J %% Byproducts and Alternatives AF --> AW{OR: Alternative
Pyruvate Fate?} AW -->|Minor| AX[Pyruvate Dehydrogenase Bypass] AW -->|Minor| AY[Amino Acid Biosynthesis] AW -->|Major| AJ %% Regulation by Glucose Sensing B --> AZ[/SNF1/Snf1 Kinase Inhibition/] AZ --> BA[/Glucose Repression Active/] BA --> BB[/Repress Respiratory Genes/] BA --> BC[Activate Fermentation Genes] BC --> J BC --> M BC --> AJ BC --> AN %% Output and Products AO --> BD[Ethanol Accumulation] AK --> BE[CO2 Release] BD --> BF{Ethanol Concentration
Toxic?} BF -->|High| BG[Ethanol Stress Response] BF -->|Normal| BH[Continue Fermentation] BG --> BI[Hsp12, Hsp26 Induction] BG --> BJ[Reduced Fermentation Rate] %% Glycerol Production (Redox Balance Alternative) T --> BK{OR: Excess NADH?} BK -->|Yes| BL[Glycerol-3-P Dehydrogenase GPD1/GPD2] BL --> BM[Glycerol Production] BM --> BN[NAD+ Regenerated] BN --> AQ %% Final Products BD --> BO[Ethanol Export] BE --> BP[CO2 Released as Gas] BM --> BQ[Glycerol as Byproduct] AR --> BR[ATP for Cellular Processes] %% Oxygen Shift Response C --> BS{Oxygen Returns?} BS -->|Yes| BT[Diauxic Shift] BT -->
